questions.q: In which Indian city is the Padmanabhaswamy Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Padmanabhaswamy Temple stands in Thiruvananthapuram, the capital city of Kerala.

questions.q: Padmanabhaswamy Temple is dedicated primarily to which Hindu deity?
questions.explanation: The temple is dedicated to Vishnu, worshipped here as Padmanabha, the lotus-naveled lord.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the Padmanabhaswamy Temple situated?
questions.explanation: The temple is located in Thiruvananthapuram, which is the capital of the southern state of Kerala.

questions.q: The presiding deity at Padmanabhaswamy Temple is shown in what posture?
questions.explanation: The main idol depicts Vishnu in 'Anantha Shayanam', the reclining posture on the serpent Anantha.

questions.q: On which serpent does the reclining Vishnu rest in the temple's main idol?
questions.explanation: The deity reclines on the great serpent Anantha, also called Adi Shesha, hence the form Anantha Padmanabha.

questions.q: The Padmanabhaswamy Temple became globally famous in 2011 for the discovery of what?
questions.explanation: In 2011, secret underground vaults containing immense quantities of gold, jewels and valuables were inventoried.

questions.q: What is the tall, ornate gateway tower of a South Indian temple like Padmanabhaswamy called?
questions.explanation: The towering, sculpted entrance tower of a Dravidian temple is known as a gopuram.

questions.q: The temple's name 'Padmanabha' refers to which feature of Vishnu?
options: A lotus rising from his navel
questions.explanation: 'Padmanabha' means 'lotus-navel', referring to the lotus emerging from Vishnu's navel bearing Brahma.

questions.q: Which body of water lies adjacent to the Padmanabhaswamy Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple stands beside the Padma Theertham, a sacred temple tank used by pilgrims.

questions.q: The Padmanabhaswamy Temple is built mainly in which architectural style?
questions.explanation: The temple is built in the Dravidian style of South Indian temple architecture, with Kerala features.

questions.q: Which royal house traditionally served as the patrons and custodians of the Padmanabhaswamy Temple?
questions.explanation: The Travancore royal family long managed the temple and styled themselves servants of the deity.

questions.q: The Travancore kings styled themselves by which title in service of the temple deity?
questions.explanation: The rulers took the title 'Padmanabha Dasa', meaning servant of Lord Padmanabha, dedicating the kingdom to him.

questions.q: Which 18th-century Travancore king is credited with reconstructing the temple and dedicating the kingdom to Padmanabha?
questions.explanation: Maharaja Marthanda Varma rebuilt the temple in the 18th century and performed the Thrippadi Danam dedicating Travancore to the deity.

questions.q: The ceremonial act by which Marthanda Varma surrendered the kingdom to Lord Padmanabha is known as what?
questions.explanation: In 1750, Marthanda Varma performed the Thrippadi Danam, dedicating Travancore to the deity and ruling as his servant.

questions.q: The main idol of Padmanabha is famously made using a special composite material known as what?
questions.explanation: The reclining idol is fashioned from 'katusarkara yogam', a hardened ayurvedic mixture rather than carved stone or metal.

questions.q: Devotees view the reclining Padmanabha idol through how many doors of the sanctum?
questions.explanation: The large reclining idol is viewed through three separate doors, showing the head, the navel with Brahma, and the feet.

questions.q: Padmanabhaswamy Temple is counted among the 108 Divya Desams, the holy abodes praised by which group of saints?
questions.explanation: It is one of the 108 Divya Desams, sacred Vishnu shrines glorified in the hymns of the Alvar saints.

questions.q: The vaults of the temple were labelled with letters; which lettered vault remained famously unopened amid controversy?
questions.explanation: Vault B was kept sealed, with concerns over a protective curse, while other vaults were opened and inventoried.

questions.q: The temple's gopuram rises through how many tiers or storeys?
questions.explanation: The principal gopuram of the temple is a seven-tiered tower in the Dravidian style.

questions.q: Which two grand biannual festivals are celebrated at the Padmanabhaswamy Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple holds two major festivals each year, the Alpashy festival and the Painkuni festival, each ending in a sea procession.

questions.q: The temple's famous pillared corridor and hall is renowned for which feature?
questions.explanation: The temple's mandapams are noted for their finely carved granite pillars, including the ornate Ottakkal Mandapam single-stone platform.

questions.q: The 2011 inventory of the temple's vaults made it widely regarded as which kind of institution?
options: One of the richest temples in the world
questions.explanation: The vast wealth found in its vaults made Padmanabhaswamy one of the wealthiest religious institutions in the world.

questions.q: The 2011 opening of the vaults followed a petition that ultimately reached which court?
questions.explanation: The matter over control and inventory of the temple's treasures was taken up by the Supreme Court of India.

questions.q: In a 2020 verdict, the Supreme Court upheld the administrative rights over the temple of which party?
questions.explanation: The 2020 Supreme Court judgment upheld the rights of the erstwhile Travancore royal family in the temple's management.

questions.q: The single-stone platform before the sanctum, used for special rituals, is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Ottakkal Mandapam is a ritual platform carved from a single block of granite placed before the main shrine.

questions.q: The reclining idol incorporates which other major deity emerging from Padmanabha's navel?
questions.explanation: From the navel of the reclining Vishnu rises a lotus bearing Brahma, the creator, viewed through the central door.

questions.q: The temple's traditional dress code requires male devotees to wear which garment?
options: A mundu (dhoti) with the upper body bare
questions.explanation: Men must enter wearing a mundu or dhoti and remove upper-body garments, following the temple's strict dress code.

questions.q: The Travancore festival in which the deity's processional images are taken for a ceremonial bath in the sea is called the what?
questions.explanation: The Arattu procession concludes the major festivals, when the processional deities are taken to the Arabian Sea for a holy bath at Shanghumukham.

questions.q: The Travancore royal title 'Padmanabha Dasa' was used by male rulers; female members of the house used which corresponding title?
questions.explanation: Female members of the Travancore royal family bore the title 'Padmanabha Sevini', the feminine counterpart of the male servant title Padmanabha Dasa.

questions.q: Which devotional sect's hymns, sung by saints whose works form the Naalayira Divya Prabandham, glorify this temple?
questions.explanation: As a Divya Desam, the temple is praised in the Naalayira Divya Prabandham, the Tamil hymns of the Vaishnava Alvar saints.
